name: ADRENAL HEMORRHAGE
definition: high concentration of intracellular deoxyhemoglobin with preferential T2 proton T1 shortening ← paramagnetic effect of free methemoglobin (Fe3+) ← produced by T2 proton relaxation enhancement ← hemosiderin deposition + presence of a fibrous
dominant_finding: round / oval mass displacing kidney inferiorly + IVC anteriorly
discriminating_features: round / oval mass displacing kidney inferiorly + IVC anteriorly
uniform adrenal enlargement gradually decreasing in size over 6-8 weeks (follow-up for 2-3

general: round / oval mass displacing kidney inferiorly + IVC anteriorly
uniform adrenal enlargement gradually decreasing in size over 6-8 weeks (follow-up for 2-3
acute stage (< 7 days):: round / oval mass (in 83%) located in medulla + stretching cortex around hematoma:
hyperattenuating (50-90 HU) similar to most neoplasms in acute stage → slowly
obliteration of gland by diffuse irregular hemorrhage (in 9%)
asymmetric thickening of diaphragmatic crus ← periadrenal hemorrhage
chronic stage (> 7 weeks):: mass with hypoattenuating center ± calcifications = adrenal pseudocyst (DDx: adrenal
complex solid heterogeneously hyperechoic mass during early stage
mixed echogenicity with centrally hypoechoic region (as liquefaction occurs)
peripheral curvilinear hyperechoic calcifications appearing within 1-2 weeks
completely anechoic / cystlike in chronic stage
avascularity on color Doppler / power Doppler
"blooming effect" (= magnetic susceptibility) of hemosiderin in gradient-echo imaging
subacute stage (7 days-7 weeks):: hyperintensity on T1WI and T2WI appearing in periphery with filling in (over several
hematoma may be multilocular, each locule with its own different signal intensity

clinical.Rx: supportive; prompt glucocorticoid administration with concomitant intravenous saline
clinical.Prognosis: (1) unilateral hematoma: spontaneous resolution without sequelae
